input_row.title: Ensure that logging for Azure AppService 'HTTP logs' is enabled
input_row.description: Enable AppServiceHTTPLogs diagnostic log category for Azure App Service instances to ensure all http requests are captured and centrally logged.
input_row.rationale: Capturing web requests can be important supporting information for security analysts performing monitoring and incident response activities. Once logging, these logs can be ingested into SIEM or other central aggregation point for the organization. Impact: Log consumption and processing will incur additional cost.
input_row.audit: Audit from Azure Portal 1. Go to App Services. For each App Service: 2. Under Monitoring, go to Diagnostic settings. 3. Ensure a diagnostic setting exists that logs HTTP logs to a destination aligned to your environment's approach to log consumption (event hub, storage account, etc. dependent on what is consuming the logs such as SIEM or other log aggregation utility). Audit from Azure Policy If referencing a digital copy of this Benchmark, clicking a Policy ID will open a link to the associated Policy definition in Azure. If referencing a printed copy, you can search Policy IDs from this URL: https://portal.azure.com/#view/Microsoft_Azure_Policy/PolicyMenuBlade/~/Definitions • Policy ID: 91a78b24-f231-4a8a-8da9-02c35b2b6510 - Name: 'App Service apps should have resource logs enabled' • Policy ID: d639b3af-a535-4bef-8dcf-15078cddf5e2 - Name: 'App Service app slots should have resource logs enabled'
input_row.remediation: Remediate from Azure Portal 1. Go to App Services. For each App Service: 2. Under Monitoring, go to Diagnostic settings. 3. To update an existing diagnostic setting, click Edit setting against the setting. To create a new diagnostic setting, click Add diagnostic setting and provide a name for the new setting. 4. Check the checkbox next to HTTP logs. 5. Configure a destination based on your specific logging consumption capability (for example Stream to an event hub and then consuming with SIEM integration for Event Hub logging). 6. Click Save. Default Value: Not configured. References: 1. https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/app-service/troubleshoot-diagnostic-logs 2. https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/security/benchmark/azure/mcsb-logging-threat- detection#lt-3-enable-logging-for-security-investigation
input_row.impact: Log consumption and processing will incur additional cost.

gpt_response:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**APPROACH DECISION:**
- The audit can be confirmed through the Azure Resource Manager API and Azure CLI commands that query the diagnostic settings of App Service resources, which include HTTP logs as one of the enabling parameters.

**PROGRAM NAME:** azure_appservice_http_logs_enabled

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
- The automated program would check the following:
  - Use the Azure Resource Manager API or Azure CLI commands to enumerate all App Service instances within specified subscriptions or resource groups.
  - For each App Service instance, query the diagnostic settings using the API endpoint `GET /subscriptions/{subscriptionId}/resourceGroups/{resourceGroupName}/providers/Microsoft.Web/sites/{name}/diagnosticSettings`.
  - Validate the presence of a diagnostic setting that enables HTTP logs specifically.
- Configuration properties to validate:
  - Ensure that `logs` property includes `httpLogs` and is correctly configured.
  - Validate that the logs are streaming to a proper destination (like an Event Hub, storage account, etc.) and that it aligns with the organization's log consumption capability.
- Clear Pass/Fail criteria:
  - Pass: If at least one diagnostic setting exists for an App Service that has `httpLogs` enabled with a valid destination.
  - Fail: If no such diagnostic setting exists or if `httpLogs` is not included in the configuration.
- Example CLI command:
  ```bash
  az webapp log deployment show --name <app-service-name> --resource-group <resource-group-name>
  ```
